participants.reasoning: The extracted participants match the official trial details on sample size, surgery type, inclusion/exclusion criteria, and overall adult OPCAB population, with only minor added detail not conflicting with the official record.
participants.extracted: Forty adult patients undergoing off-pump coronary artery bypass (OPCAB) surgery were enrolled and randomized into two groups: 20 received continuous milrinone infusion and 20 received saline control. Eligible participants had stenosis in more than three coronary arteries and normal preoperative ventricular function (left and right ventricular ejection fraction at least 40%). Patients with valvular heart disease, severe cerebral or renal dysfunction, emergency surgery, or significant ventricular dysfunction were excluded. The study population consisted of aged adult cardiac surgery patients.

Inclusion criteria:stenosis at more than 3 coronary arteries
Exclusion criteria:emergency surgery; LVEF or RVEF < 40%; valvular heart disease; severe cerebral/renal dysfuncti

interventions.reasoning: The extracted intervention matches the official milrinone dose and timing during coronary artery anastomosis, and the control saline corresponds to placebo.
interventions.extracted: Continuous intravenous milrinone infusion at 0.5 µg/kg/min, started after sternotomy and continued during coronary artery anastomosis in patients undergoing off-pump coronary artery bypass surgery. The control group received normal saline.
interventions.official:
Intervention:milrinone 0.5 µg/kg/min during coronary artery anastomosis
Comparison:placebo
Concomitant medication:norepinephrine
Excluded medications:no information given

outcomes.reasoning: The extracted outcomes match the official primary outcomes and time points, with only minor wording differences and some additional clinically related outcomes not conflicting with the official record.
outcomes.extracted: The trial planned to measure hemodynamic and right ventricular function outcomes during OPCAB and early postoperative outcomes. Hemodynamic outcomes included heart rate, mean arterial pressure, central venous pressure, mean pulmonary artery pressure, pulmonary capillary wedge pressure, mixed venous oxygen saturation, cardiac output/cardiac index, systemic vascular resistance index, and pulmonary vascular resistance index, measured after pericardiotomy (baseline, T1), at 5 minutes after stabilizer application during left anterior descending artery anastomosis (T2), obtuse marginal artery anastomosis (T3), and posterior descending artery/right coronary artery anastomosis (T4), and 5 minutes after sternum closure (T5), with additional ICU measurements immediately on arrival, and at 6 and 12 hours after ICU admission. Right ventricular volumetric outcomes included right ventricular ejection fraction, right ventricular end-systolic volume index, and right ventricular end-diastolic volume index, measured at the same intraoperative and ICU time points. Early clinical outcomes planned for measurement were permanent cerebral infarction/stroke within 30 days after surgery, renal insufficiency/renal failure, mechanical ventilation lasting more than 48 hours, deep sternal infection, re-operation, myocardial infarction, and mortality/death, assessed during the early postoperative period and up to 30 days after operation.
outcomes.official:
Primary:haemodynamics; right ventricular function; short‐term clinical outcomes (cerebrovascular event, renal failure, prolonged ventilation (> 48 hours), reoperation, sternal infection)
Secondary:no information given
Time points reported:preoperative; after pericardiotomy; 5 min each after stabiliser application for anastomosis of specific arteries; at the end of surgery; 6/12 hours after admission to ICU; postoperative day 30
